title: Coreopsis multiligulata
taxonomy: Coreopsis multiligulata is a species within the genus Coreopsis and family Asteraceae. This classification places it in the order Asterales and the clade called core eudicots. Coreopsis multiligulata further belongs to the clade of flowering plants, or angiosperms, and the larger clade of angiosperm-like plants called magnoliophytes. As a member of the aster family, Coreopsis multiligulata has relatives like daisies and sunflowers. Coreopsis multiligulata's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in its family.
uses: Coreopsis multiligulata is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States. Coreopsis multiligulata has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with its roots and leaves believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties. It has also been used to treat various ailments, such as fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. In some Native American cultures, the plant is used to treat respiratory problems and to soothe skin irritations. Further studies are needed to confirm the efficacy of Coreopsis multiligulata in modern medicine.
